fields.summary: Specifies transfer function characteristics as defined by CICP (Coding-Independent Code Points, ITU-T H.273).
fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp` enumerates the transfer function (opto-electronic or
electro-optical) values defined by the CICP specification (ITU-T H.273 / ISO/IEC 23091-2).
These values describe how scene light is encoded into signal values.

Use these values with <xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorSpace.CreateCicp(SkiaSharp.SKColorspacePrimariesCicp,SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp)>
to create a color space from standard CICP parameters.

## Examples

```csharp
var colorSpace = SKColorSpace.CreateCicp(
    SKColorspacePrimariesCicp.Rec2020,
    SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp.Pq);
```
]]></format>

fields.summary: Hybrid Log-Gamma (HLG) transfer function for HDR content (CICP code 18).
Linear transfer function with no gamma encoding (CICP code 8).
Perceptual Quantizer (PQ) transfer function for HDR content (CICP code 16).
ITU-R BT.2020 transfer function for 10-bit content (CICP code 14).
ITU-R BT.2020 transfer function for 12-bit content (CICP code 15).
ITU-R BT.470 System B/G transfer function with assumed gamma 2.8 (CICP code 5).
ITU-R BT.470 System M transfer function with assumed gamma 2.2 (CICP code 4).
ITU-R BT.601 transfer function for standard definition content (CICP code 6).
ITU-R BT.709 transfer function for high definition content (CICP code 1).
SMPTE ST 240 transfer function for early HDTV content (CICP code 7).
Unknown or unspecified transfer function (CICP code 0).
